title: Welsh oven-bottom muffins
subtitle: Soft, yeast-leavened griddle muffins cooked on a bakestone
excerpt: Soft, yeast-leavened muffins cooked on a heavy griddle or bakestone rather than in the oven, producing a characteristic pale, floury top and bottom with a golden equator around the middle. The dough is enriched with a little butter and milk and proved before cooking. Split open and toasted, they have a chewy, slightly dense crumb that holds butter well.
sourceNotes: Oven-bottom muffins (also called Welsh muffins or crumpet-muffins) are documented in Welsh and English baking literature from the eighteenth century as a standard bakestone-cooked bread. Hannah Glasse, The Art of Cookery Made Plain and Easy (1747, fully in the public domain and available at Project Gutenberg), describes muffins as a yeast-leavened griddle bread. The pale, floury exterior from cooking on a well-dusted bakestone or griddle is described as the defining characteristic in baking guides published before 1928.

content.text: Welsh oven-bottom muffins are cooked entirely on the hob, with no oven involved. The key is
getting the griddle to the right temperature
before the muffins go on. Too hot and the surfaces scorch while the dough stays raw inside; the muffin should cook through in about 12 minutes on each side over medium-low heat.

Mix the flour, yeast, salt, and sugar in a large bowl. Add the warm milk, water, and melted butter and mix to a soft dough. Knead for 8 minutes until smooth. Cover and leave for 60 minutes until doubled. Tip onto a well-floured surface and press gently to about 2.5 cm thick. Cut into rounds using a large cutter (about 8 to 9 cm) or a glass dipped in flour. Place on a floured tray, dust the tops generously with flour, cover lightly, and prove for 30 minutes.

Heat the griddle over medium-low heat. Cook the muffins in batches without oil, spacing them well apart. Cook for 10 to 12 minutes on the first side until pale golden at the edges, then flip and cook for another 10 to 12 minutes. The sides should look fully set and the muffin should spring back when pressed. Cool on a rack before splitting and toasting.
